title: Switching Axis Registry
role: Authoritative vocabulary of filterable axes for the Networking Equipment Selector. The registry enforces MEANING (what can be constrained, how, and which values are legal). It is the SOURCE; the KB schema is a projection of it (every axis implies a typed, validated field on a model entry). Nothing is filterable unless declared here. This file is the single source of truth for axis enum legal_values; validate2.py checks model values against it.
consumers: Web UI builds form controls from this registry.
MCP server (Stage 2) builds tool parameters from this registry.
Reasoning agent (Stage 3) reasons over this registry (via the MCP server).
The KB JSON schema projects typed fields from these axes.
change_policy: Adding a switch model is CHEAP (more data the solver already handles). Adding or changing an AXIS is EXPENSIVE and STRUCTURAL: it touches the UI, the MCP parameters, possibly a new solver condition type. An agent populating models must NEVER add, rename, or re-type an axis on its own — surface it for human ruling. Bump registry_version when the axis set changes.

axes.name: series
axes.notes: Series is an ordinary axis, not a privileged root. Sub-families are distinct series values (e.g. C9300LM does not stack with C9300 modular SKUs, uses different PSUs/stacking). Legal_values will grow as families are added.

axes.name: poe_type
axes.notes: Per-type wattage (poe 15.4W / poe+ 30W / upoe 60W / upoe+ 90W) is a fixed standard, handled as a frontend display lookup, not a model field or axis.

axes.name: poe_budget_watts
axes.notes: Optional: present only when poe_capable is true. Value = max achievable across the switch's poe_budget_matrix (the PSU-pair that delivers a requested wattage is resolved later from that matrix).

axes.name: poe_port_count
axes.notes: Optional: present when poe_capable is true.

axes.name: multigig_port_count
axes.notes: Count of access ports capable of >1G (Multigigabit). 0 for non-mGig models.

axes.name: access_1g_fiber
axes.notes: Count of FIBER/SFP access ports that handle 1G. Optional: present only on models with fiber access ports. A single physical port that handles multiple speeds is counted under EACH speed it supports, so access_1g_fiber / access_10g_fiber / access_25g_fiber may overlap on the same ports (e.g. 12x SFP28 ports each doing 1/10/25G count 12 on all three). Distinct from multigig_port_count, which is COPPER (RJ-45) Multigigabit. The JSON declares the real per-speed capability per the design decision that the solver holds no 25G-implies-10G rule; the solver only flat-matches a requested speed against the declared counts.

axes.name: access_10g_fiber
axes.notes: Count of FIBER/SFP access ports that handle 10G. Optional; present only on models with fiber access ports. Same overlap rule as access_1g_fiber. Copper Multigigabit 10G is counted under multigig_port_count, not here.

axes.name: access_25g_fiber
axes.notes: Count of FIBER/SFP access ports that handle 25G. Optional; present only on models with fiber access ports. Same overlap rule as access_1g_fiber.

axes.name: uplink_modular
axes.notes: true: uplinks come from a network module (the switch references a module group whose uplink_capacity block supplies the per-speed values). false: uplinks fixed in SKU (the switch carries an inline uplink_capacity block of the same shape).

axes.name: uplink_1g
axes.notes: Per-speed uplink axis. NOT a precomputed model value: resolves against uplink_capacity.per_speed_max (from the referenced module group on modular models, or the inline block on fixed models). per_speed_max = max provided by a single module/bay; with bay_count 1 the speeds are mutually exclusive (you fit one module).

axes.name: uplink_mgig
axes.notes: Multigigabit uplink count. Resolves against uplink_capacity.per_speed_max.

axes.name: uplink_100g
axes.notes: Resolves against uplink_capacity.per_speed_max. Absent from C9300-NM modules (no member provides 100G); axis retained for C9300X-class / fixed models that do.

axes.name: stacking_technology
axes.notes: Required and non-blank on a model when stacking_capable is true. May be left unconstrained (blank) in a QUERY (user filters stacking_capable without pinning technology).

axes.name: license_regime
axes.notes: Set of regimes a model OFFERS. First-class user-selectable input. Couples to switch selection as an ordinary hard constraint (e.g. meraki-subscription -> -M models only). Cisco Catalyst (DNX) program is intentionally NOT a regime. Each regime maps to a shape (term-based / subscription-based) on the license entries.
